name: quota-keeper
description: QuotaKeeper protects long-running Codex Goals with explicit .goalkeeper quota usage limits, checkpointing progress, pausing near quota exhaustion, and optionally restarting when capacity returns.

interface.shortDescription: Pause and restart Codex Goals before quota exhaustion.
interface.longDescription: QuotaKeeper reads .goalkeeper from the supervised Codex project or asks for explicit 5-hour quota usage percent, weekly quota usage percent, and manual/automatic restart mode. It monitors Codex rate-limit capacity, not token-context usage, checkpoints progress, pauses long-running Goals before quota exhaustion, marks Goals blocked when required policy answers are missing, and optionally restarts them when capacity returns.
interface.defaultPrompt: Use QuotaKeeper to read .goalkeeper from the supervised Codex project, or ask for explicit 5-hour quota usage percent, weekly quota usage percent, and manual/automatic restart mode. This is Codex quota/rate-limit usage, not token-context usage. Do not infer defaults. If the user has not provided all three answers, tell them what is missing, then use the native Goal-control tool to mark the active Goal blocked instead of continuing the loop. Then monitor this Goal and pause or restart it according to that policy.
